etymology.description: semantic 艸(艸) (cǎo, "grass") + phonetic 胡(胡) (hú) -> related to plants, referencing the bottle gourd
etymology.interpretation: The character 葫 (hú, "bottle gourd") combines the 艸(艸) (cǎo, "grass") component, indicating a plant, with the phonetic 胡(胡) (hú), which provides the sound. The connection with vegetation through 艸, coupled with a matching sound from 胡, refers to the bottle gourd, a type of plant.

examples.chinese: 他的花园里有许多葫芦(他的花園裡有許多葫蘆)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de huāyuán lǐ yǒu xǔduō húlu.
examples.english: There are many bottle gourds in his garden.

examples.chinese: 我们用葫芦做了一个灯笼(我們用葫蘆做了一個燈籠)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yòng húlu zuòle yī gè dēnglóng.
examples.english: We made a lantern out of a bottle gourd.

examples.chinese: 葫芦不仅可以食用，还可以用来装饰(葫蘆不僅可以食用，還可以用來裝飾)。
examples.pinyin: Húlu bùjǐn kěyǐ shíyòng, hái kěyǐ yòng lái zhuāngshì.
examples.english: Bottle gourds can be eaten as well as used for decoration.
